name: Mantis Beings
description: Mantis Beings, also known as Insectoids, are a recurring theme in ufology, conspiracy theories, and certain New Age spiritual beliefs. Proponents describe them as a type of extraterrestrial or extradimensional entity characterized by a humanoid form with insect-like features, particularly resembling praying mantises. Claims about their existence range from covert interactions with humans to active manipulation of global events. Alleged encounters often involve abduction scenarios, genetic experimentation, and psychic communication. Origin stories vary; some theories suggest they are ancient inhabitants of Earth, while others posit they originate from distant star systems. Evidence cited often includes alleged eyewitness accounts, recovered memories (sometimes through hypnosis), and interpretations of ancient art and mythology. Mainstream science and academia largely dismiss these claims, citing a lack of verifiable physical evidence and attributing purported sightings to misidentification, psychological phenomena, or deliberate hoaxes. Skeptics also point to the subjective and often inconsistent nature of the accounts, and the susceptibility of memory to suggestion, particularly in the context of hypnosis. The concept's popularity is fueled by science fiction, online communities, and a general fascination with the unknown.
subtitle: Insectoid extraterrestrials in ufology and theory.

claims.claim: Mantis Beings are a real extraterrestrial species.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts of encounters and abductions.
claims.criticism: Lack of physical evidence; potential for misidentification, hallucination, or fabricated stories.

claims.claim: Mantis Beings are conducting genetic experiments on humans.
claims.evidence: Reports of medical examinations and procedures during alleged abductions.
claims.criticism: Skepticism regarding recovered memories under hypnosis; similarity to science fiction tropes.

claims.claim: Mantis Beings are influencing global events and human society.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of ancient texts and symbols, alleged insider testimonies.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence; susceptibility to confirmation bias and pareidolia.

keyFigures.name: David Icke
keyFigures.role: Promoter of reptilian and insectoid conspiracy theories

keyFigures.name: Linda Moulton Howe
keyFigures.role: Investigative reporter focusing on UFOs and cattle mutilations (sometimes linked to Mantis Beings)

keyFigures.name: Carl Sagan
keyFigures.role: Prominent skeptic and science communicator who emphasized the need for extraordinary evidence for extraordinary claims

timeline.event: Increased reports of alleged alien encounters and abductions, including descriptions resembling Mantis Beings.
Rise of New Age spirituality and interest in extraterrestrial contact, contributing to the popularization of Mantis Being theories.
Continued online discussion and speculation about Mantis Beings in UFO and conspiracy theory communities.

sources.author: Budd Hopkins
sources.work: Intruders: The Incredible Visitations at Copley Woods
sources.title: Budd Hopkins

sources.author: Susan Clancy
sources.work: Abducted: How People Come to Believe They Were Kidnapped by Aliens
sources.title: Susan Clancy
